latest.leading_indicator: Detection of coordinated inauthentic accounts using LLM-agent coordination signatures concentrated in specific data-center permitting jurisdictions; or a sudden, geographically clustered surge in anti-siting organizing whose linguistic fingerprints suggest multi-persona automation around a single project.

risk: Persistent-memory agent infrastructure (Always-On Agents, Memora, Neural Procedural Memory) is being shipped as a capability while the only safety subfield that studies durable agent state — Self-Improvement Risk & Control — is decelerating, leaving memory poisoning and slow cross-session drift as an unmonitored attack surface in the exact long-horizon agents now entering consumer deployment.

latest.risk: Persistent-memory agent infrastructure (Always-On Agents, Memora, Neural Procedural Memory) is being shipped as a capability while the only safety subfield that studies durable agent state — Self-Improvement Risk & Control — is decelerating, leaving memory poisoning and slow cross-session drift as an unmonitored attack surface in the exact long-horizon agents now entering consumer deployment.
latest.leading_indicator: Documented memory-poisoning incidents where an agent's behavior changes across sessions from content injected earlier; vendor patches adding 'memory integrity' or 'memory rollback' controls; red-team disclosures of cross-session persistence attacks on Gemini computer-use or OpenAI agent memory.

risk: Multilingual auditor-judge safety benchmarks (LLM-grades-LLM) for emotional-support and child-safety chatbots are arriving exactly as the safety-research subfield they depend on (Scalable Oversight) DECELERATES — meaning the 'judge' models doing multi-turn crisis grading rest on an oversight foundation whose methodological scrutiny is shrinking just as these benchmarks get drafted into the one legally-carved-out domain (child safety).

latest.risk: Multilingual auditor-judge safety benchmarks (LLM-grades-LLM) for emotional-support and child-safety chatbots are arriving exactly as the safety-research subfield they depend on (Scalable Oversight) DECELERATES — meaning the 'judge' models doing multi-turn crisis grading rest on an oversight foundation whose methodological scrutiny is shrinking just as these benchmarks get drafted into the one legally-carved-out domain (child safety).
latest.leading_indicator: An auditor-judge benchmark (EMPATH/CAREBench lineage) cited in a CAISI evaluation protocol, a state child-safety rule, or a lab system card as the child-safety standard — WITHOUT a published judge-reliability / judge-gameability validation across the benchmark's claimed languages.

risk: Reward Hacking and Self-Improvement Risk research are both DECELERATING just as OpenAI/Broadcom ship LLM-optimized inference chips and agentic products optimize for engagement/task-completion at scale — meaning the specification-gaming literature is contracting precisely when economic incentives to deploy outcome-optimized agents into low-trust consumer markets are peaking.

latest.risk: Reward Hacking and Self-Improvement Risk research are both DECELERATING just as OpenAI/Broadcom ship LLM-optimized inference chips and agentic products optimize for engagement/task-completion at scale — meaning the specification-gaming literature is contracting precisely when economic incentives to deploy outcome-optimized agents into low-trust consumer markets are peaking.
latest.leading_indicator: Consumer-protection complaints or FTC inquiries about agentic assistants steering users toward outcomes that benefit the operator (upsells, locked-in bookings) rather than the user; OR product safety docs that cite reward-hacking benchmarks more than ~18 months old because nothing newer was published.

risk: Training-free, confidence-driven test-time RL on robot/embodied (VLA) policies lets deployed physical agents rewrite their own behavior using self-generated reward signals — outside any weight-update audit trail and outside the bounded-task monitoring that physical-safety regulators actually run.

latest.risk: Training-free, confidence-driven test-time RL on robot/embodied (VLA) policies lets deployed physical agents rewrite their own behavior using self-generated reward signals — outside any weight-update audit trail and outside the bounded-task monitoring that physical-safety regulators actually run.
latest.leading_indicator: First incident report or recall in which a deployed robot/agent's behavior is found to have changed post-deployment via on-device adaptation with no corresponding firmware/weight update record; or a robotics vendor system card disclosing 'continuous test-time adaptation' without an accompanying re-certification protocol.

risk: 'Thinking-intervention' and refusal-decoding research borrowing alignment work is quietly building a reusable toolkit to extract refused knowledge from aligned models — turning the post-deployment monitoring pivot into an offense-enabling layer just as CAISI certifies models on bio/chem/cyber refusals.

latest.risk: 'Thinking-intervention' and refusal-decoding research borrowing alignment work is quietly building a reusable toolkit to extract refused knowledge from aligned models — turning the post-deployment monitoring pivot into an offense-enabling layer just as CAISI certifies models on bio/chem/cyber refusals.
latest.leading_indicator: A published demonstration that a CAISI-evaluated or open-weight frontier model's certified dangerous-capability refusals are reliably recovered via linear decoding or thinking-intervention on a held-out bio/chem/cyber prompt set, OR a CAISI methodology note acknowledging refusal-decoding as an evasion class.

risk: Robot manipulation policies are being validated with offline proxy metrics whose 'critical interval' weighting is researcher-chosen, letting unsafe physical-world VLA policies pass review precisely where harm concentrates.

latest.risk: Robot manipulation policies are being validated with offline proxy metrics whose 'critical interval' weighting is researcher-chosen, letting unsafe physical-world VLA policies pass review precisely where harm concentrates.
latest.leading_indicator: Adoption of 'critical-interval'-style offline metrics as the reported validation in commercial VLA/robot-manipulation product datasheets or procurement specs, without an accompanying real-world worst-case test — or a physical-injury incident traced to a policy that passed offline review.

risk: Child-safety and emotional-support chatbot benchmarks are arriving precisely as child safety is carved OUT of federal preemption — turning a thin, fresh research literature into the de facto legal standard before it is mature, with no cross-lingual deployment validation.

latest.risk: Child-safety and emotional-support chatbot benchmarks are arriving precisely as child safety is carved OUT of federal preemption — turning a thin, fresh research literature into the de facto legal standard before it is mature, with no cross-lingual deployment validation.
latest.leading_indicator: A regulator, platform child-safety policy, or the EU Article 5 implementing guidance referencing a specific child-safety chatbot benchmark as the conformance bar without requiring multilingual/multi-turn coverage; OR a documented harm in a non-English emotional-support chatbot interaction that passed an adopted English-centric benchmark.

risk: Mechanistic-interpretability techniques are quietly being repurposed as embedding-space anomaly detectors for cyber-physical and decoding-fault settings, creating a fragile single-signal trust layer that adversaries can spoof faster than defenders can validate it.

latest.risk: Mechanistic-interpretability techniques are quietly being repurposed as embedding-space anomaly detectors for cyber-physical and decoding-fault settings, creating a fragile single-signal trust layer that adversaries can spoof faster than defenders can validate it.
latest.leading_indicator: Reference to embedding-dimension or 'behavioral characterization' anomaly detectors in a deployed SOC/ICS monitoring product or a published adversarial-evasion result showing such detectors fooled by in-distribution-preserving perturbations.

risk: Generative molecular-design benchmarks are deliberately moving beyond drug-likeness into structurally novel chemical spaces (nanomaterials) just as biosecurity evaluation infrastructure remains anchored to drug/bio proxies — opening a dual-use chem-design seam that current pre-deployment evals are not built to screen.

latest.leading_indicator: A published generative-design model or benchmark optimizing for properties (toxicity, stability, dispersal) in non-drug chemical space without dual-use review, or a CAISI/lab eval finding that existing chem screens fail to flag off-distribution optimization targets.

risk: Privacy-preserving and confidential-inference techniques being borrowed into agentic deployments are closing off the very inspection surfaces that pre-deployment government evaluators (CAISI) and incident-reporting regimes (SB 53) implicitly assume they can access.

latest.risk: Privacy-preserving and confidential-inference techniques being borrowed into agentic deployments are closing off the very inspection surfaces that pre-deployment government evaluators (CAISI) and incident-reporting regimes (SB 53) implicitly assume they can access.
latest.leading_indicator: A frontier lab or enterprise vendor cites privacy/confidentiality (or trade-secret protection of context) as the reason a CAISI or SB-53-driven evaluation could not observe a specific agentic execution path; or a safety-incident report under SB 53 notes that root-cause analysis was blocked by confidential-inference state being unrecoverable.

risk: Decision-attribution explainability tools are arriving for predict-then-optimize systems just as US preemption strips state algorithmic-accountability law — creating tools that can prove the wrong thing to courts that no longer require the right thing.

latest.risk: Decision-attribution explainability tools are arriving for predict-then-optimize systems just as US preemption strips state algorithmic-accountability law — creating tools that can prove the wrong thing to courts that no longer require the right thing.
latest.leading_indicator: ADMT-compliance audits or litigation where a system passes explainability requirements via forecast attribution yet the contested decision is shown to be driven by the (unexplained) optimization step; or standards bodies (NIST/CAISI) adopting forecast-level explainability without distinguishing decision-value attribution.
